duo.modalTitle: Which accounts do you want to add?

diversif.title: Diversification
diversif.body: Don't put all your eggs in one basket. If you have 10 companies across different sectors and one goes bankrupt, you lose 10%. Diversification spreads the risk.
diversif.tip: Do you have more than 30% of your portfolio in a single sector?

dca.title: Dollar Cost Averaging
dca.body: Investing the same amount every month, regardless of whether the market goes up or down. When it drops, you buy more shares for the same price. Discipline beats timing.
dca.tip: Set a fixed monthly amount and automate it.

dividendo.title: Dividends
dividendo.body: Some companies pay you just for holding their shares. Automatically reinvesting dividends can double your return over 20 years.
dividendo.tip: Look for companies with 10+ years of consecutive dividend increases.

pe_ratio.title: P/E Ratio
pe_ratio.body: The P/E compares a stock's price to what the company earns. There's no universal 'good' number — compare it against the sector and its history.
pe_ratio.tip: A very high P/E doesn't mean expensive if the company is growing fast. Context beats the number.

moat.title: Competitive Advantage
moat.body: The 'moat' is what makes a company hard to copy: brand (Apple), network (Visa), low costs (Walmart). It protects its margins over time.
moat.tip: What would stop a competitor with $1B from taking this market?

aversion.title: Loss Aversion
aversion.body: The brain feels the pain of losing $100 twice as strongly as the pleasure of gaining $100. That's why we sell in a panic. Recognizing this bias is the first step.
aversion.tip: Before selling on a dip: did the business fundamentals actually change?

rebalanceo.title: Rebalancing
rebalanceo.body: If your stocks went up a lot, you now have more risk than you wanted. Rebalancing means selling what grew and buying what fell behind.
rebalanceo.tip: Rebalance 1-2 times a year, or whenever something drifts more than 5% from your target.

fund.title: Free Cash Flow
fund.body: Earnings can be manipulated through accounting. Free Cash Flow doesn't lie — it's the real cash coming into the company's coffers.
fund.tip: Look for companies whose FCF grows faster than their revenue.

horizonte.title: Investment Horizon
horizonte.body: The market drops 10-20% about once every 1-2 years. But over 10+ years, the S&P 500 has never lost money. Your horizon determines how much risk you can absorb.
horizonte.tip: Need the money in less than 3 years? It shouldn't be in stocks.

fomo.title: Financial FOMO
fomo.body: Fear Of Missing Out makes you buy at the top. FOMO is the most reliable signal that an asset is overvalued. The best time to buy is when nobody's talking about it.
fomo.tip: If it's all over the news and your family's WhatsApp — be careful.

etf.title: ETFs vs Stocks
etf.body: An ETF replicates an entire index. 90% of active managers do NOT beat the index over 15 years. ETFs are the smart starting point.
etf.tip: Start with broad ETFs. Add individual stocks only once you understand the business.

comprar_negocios.title: Buy Businesses
comprar_negocios.body: Before buying a stock: do I understand how this company makes money? Would I still be buying if the market closed for 5 years?
comprar_negocios.tip: Warren Buffett only invests in what he can understand in 5 minutes.

bull_bear.title: Drops Are Normal
bull_bear.body: Since 1950, the S&P 500 has fallen more than 10% about once a year on average. Despite that, it multiplied 200x. Drops are the price of returns.
bull_bear.tip: Keep a list of companies you'd want to buy when they fall.

concentracion.title: Concentration Risk
concentracion.body: More than 20% in a single company or more than 40% in one sector is high concentration. If that company collapses, so does your portfolio.
concentracion.tip: Calculate your largest position. Is it more than 20% of your portfolio?
